description:
## Background

Once Mermaid Diagrams render safely, readers need to inspect large diagrams without losing their place in the surrounding Issue Detail. Mermaid does not provide a documented pan/zoom interaction API, so navigation must be managed at Beadsmith's mounted SVG boundary.

## What to build

Make every rendered Mermaid Diagram directly navigable: drag to pan, Ctrl/Command + scroll to zoom, and provide visible keyboard-accessible zoom-in, zoom-out, and **Reset / fit diagram** controls. The initial and reset viewport fits the full diagram. The Source tab remains the reader's selectable/copyable representation.

## Technical Approach

- Add `@panzoom/panzoom` at the Diagram component boundary after Mermaid inserts its SVG.
- Own each pan/zoom instance with the Diagram lifecycle. Destroy it when a diagram unmounts, its source changes, or its rendered view is replaced; never leave pointer handlers on stale SVGs.
- Bind direct pointer drag to panning. Enable wheel zoom only with Ctrl/Command, leaving normal wheel movement for Issue Detail scrolling. Preserve normal touch/pinch behavior when supported by the library.
- Provide semantic buttons for zoom in, zoom out, and reset/fit. Do not make drag or wheel the only access path.
- Retain the existing strict Mermaid policy: no Mermaid links, callbacks, or other author-defined interactions compete with pan/zoom.

## Considerations

- Rendered SVG text does not need drag selection because direct drag owns panning; unchanged selectable Mermaid Source is available in the Source tab.
- Reset must restore the same full-diagram view used on first render, not merely a default library transform.
- Avoid trapping unmodified page scrolling over a diagram.
- A pan/zoom failure must not hide the already-rendered diagram or its Source view.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A reader can drag a rendered Diagram to pan it.
- [ ] Ctrl/Command + scroll zooms a Diagram; unmodified scrolling continues the Issue Detail.
- [ ] Visible accessible controls zoom in, zoom out, and restore the fit-to-diagram view.
- [ ] The initial view and reset view fit the complete rendered diagram.
- [ ] Pan/zoom resources and handlers are cleaned up on tab/source/lifecycle changes.
- [ ] Source remains reachable and selectable regardless of Diagram interaction state.

## Test Plan

- Mock the pan/zoom boundary to assert creation, modifier-gated wheel behavior, button commands, reset, and destruction.
- Test accessible names and keyboard operation for controls and tabs.
- Run focused Diagram/Markdown tests plus `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, and Ultracite checks.
title: Navigate Mermaid Diagrams with pan and zoom
